req.title: Hr-Admin deletes expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers hr-admin deleting an expense-claim via UI with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and proper HTTP responses. Tests are designed using EP (valid deletion flow), NEG (unauthenticated and forbidden-role paths), BVA (expense-claim ID at boundary/edge), STC (state transition from existing to deleted), and DT (decision table covering auth/role combinations). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own data.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_expense_claim_successfully
preconditions: A valid expense-claim record exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /expense-claims as authenticated hr-admin
Locate the target expense-claim by ID
Click the delete action for that expense-claim
Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Assert the response and UI state
expected: HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Response body contains the deleted entity id
Expense-claim no longer appears in the list
An audit record is written for the deletion
trace: R-WEB-043: audit record written on success
R-WEB-043: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_deletes_expense_claim_successfully(
    hr_admin_page,
    create_expense_claim,
    audit_log_client,
):
    claim = create_expense_claim()
    claim_id = claim["id"]

    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/expense-claims")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='claim-row-{claim_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/expense-claims/{claim_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
        page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(claim_id)

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-testid='claim-row-{claim_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_expense_claim
preconditions: No active session / user is unauthenticated
A valid expense-claim record exists in the system
actions: Attempt to send DELETE request to /expense-claims/{id} without authentication token
Assert the response status code
expected: HTTP response status is 401
Expense-claim record is not deleted
No audit record is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_expense_claim(
    base_url,
    create_expense_claim_direct,
    audit_log_client,
):
    claim = create_expense_claim_direct()
    claim_id = claim["id"]

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers={},
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401

    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=<admin_auth_headers>,
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_expense_claim
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not hr-admin)
A valid expense-claim record exists in the system
actions: Authenticate as a user with role employee
Attempt to send DELETE request to /expense-claims/{id} with employee auth token
Assert the response status code
expected: HTTP response status is 403
Expense-claim record is not deleted
No audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-043: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_expense_claim(
    base_url,
    employee_auth_headers,
    create_expense_claim_direct,
    audit_log_client,
):
    claim = create_expense_claim_direct()
    claim_id = claim["id"]

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403

    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=<admin_auth_headers>,
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0

tests.name: test_delete_expense_claim_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: An expense-claim exists whose ID is the minimum valid integer (e.g., 1)
actions: Ensure an expense-claim with minimum boundary ID exists (create or verify)
Send DELETE request to /expense-claims/1 with hr-admin credentials
Assert response status and audit entry
expected: HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Response body contains id equal to the minimum boundary value
Expense-claim with that ID is no longer retrievable
An audit record is written for the deletion
trace: R-WEB-043: BVA on expense-claim ID lower boundary
R-WEB-043: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-043: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MIN_CLAIM_ID = 1

def test_delete_expense_claim_with_minimum_valid_id(
    base_url,
    hr_admin_auth_headers,
    ensure_expense_claim_exists,
    audit_log_client,
):
    ensure_expense_claim_exists(claim_id=MIN_CLAIM_ID)

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{MIN_CLAIM_ID}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(MIN_CLAIM_ID)

    gone_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{MIN_CLAIM_ID}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
    )
    assert gone_resp.status_code == 404

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        entity_id=MIN_CLAIM_ID, action="DELETE"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_expense_claim_state_transitions_active_to_deleted
preconditions: A new expense-claim has been created and is in ACTIVE state
actions: Verify expense-claim is in ACTIVE state via GET
Navigate to /expense-claims and delete the claim via UI
Confirm deletion dialog
Verify expense-claim transitions to DELETED state or returns 404
expected: Before deletion: expense-claim status is ACTIVE (200 on GET)
After deletion: expense-claim is unreachable or status is DELETED (404 or status=DELETED)
State transition ACTIVE -> DELETED is recorded in audit log
trace: R-WEB-043: state transition ACTIVE to DELETED
R-WEB-043: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_expense_claim_state_transitions_active_to_deleted(
    base_url,
    hr_admin_page,
    hr_admin_auth_headers,
    create_expense_claim,
    audit_log_client,
):
    claim = create_expense_claim()
    claim_id = claim["id"]

    pre_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
    )
    assert pre_resp.status_code == 200
    assert pre_resp.json().get("status") == "ACTIVE"

    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/expense-claims")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='claim-row-{claim_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    expect(row).not_to_be_visible()

    post_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code in (404, 200)
    if post_resp.status_code == 200:
        assert post_resp.json().get("status") == "DELETED"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=claim_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_delete_nonexistent_expense_claim_returns_400_or_404
preconditions: The target expense-claim ID does not exist in the system
actions: Send DELETE request to /expense-claims/{nonexistent_id} with hr-admin credentials
Assert the response status code is 400 or 404
Assert no audit record is written
expected: HTTP response status is 400 or 404
Response body contains an error message
No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-043: error pathway 400 on validation error
R-WEB-043: audit record only written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

NONEXISTENT_CLAIM_ID = 999999999

def test_delete_nonexistent_expense_claim_returns_400_or_404(
    base_url,
    hr_admin_auth_headers,
    audit_log_client,
):
    pre_check = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{NONEXISTENT_CLAIM_ID}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
    )
    assert pre_check.status_code == 404, "Precondition: claim must not exist"

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{NONEXISTENT_CLAIM_ID}",
        headers=hr_admin_auth_headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code in (400, 404)
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body or "detail" in body

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        entity_id=NONEXISTENT_CLAIM_ID, action="DELETE"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0
